mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue beats that lift equity prices within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and housing starts signals broad-based economic momentum that reduces recession risk premiums, causing equity multiples to expand over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints force input costs higher, compressing profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of INDPRO contraction episodes, indicating the supply shock does not transmit to equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and credit availability continuously fuel consumer expenditure, providing a steady earnings growth tailwind that lifts equity prices over short weekly horizons regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: The rolling 6-month Spearman correlation between RSAFS momentum and 5-day SPX returns never exceeds 0.15 across the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive policy tightening that raises discount rates and curtails speculative equity valuations within one month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances following a 0.25pp rate hike threshold breach, disproving the discount rate transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong nonfarm payroll growth and declining unemployment rates validate a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle that sustains corporate hiring and wage-driven consumption, boosting quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative predictive R-squared over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference between high/low signal regimes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation readings exceeding 2.5 percent annually during an industrial output expansion indicate overheating demand that triggers premature central bank tightening, causing immediate equity risk-off positioning over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly lower during high-inflation/strong-output periods compared to baseline (t-statistic > -1.5), indicating markets price in accommodative policy.
